mechanism_narrative: A negative trailing 3-month change in FEDFUNDS lowers discount-rate expectations and supports equity duration, so larger declines in FEDFUNDS predict positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:2013-10-15 snapshot FEDFUNDS was 0.08 percent as of t-1mo
regime_hint: monetary easing or zero-rate policy regime
failure_condition: Reject if months with negative 3-month FEDFUNDS changes do not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than months with positive changes, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 for 3 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating household income and recession risk, causing investors to reduce expected earnings and equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:2013-10-15 snapshot UNRATE was 7.3 percent as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of walk-forward observations or if mean triggered returns exceed unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both rise over 3 months, consumption demand and wage-income support reinforce each other, leading to upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month RSAFS growth is positive and 3-month PAYEMS change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:2013-10-15 snapshot RSAFS was 426563 and PAYEMS was 136133 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive RSAFS-PAYEMS condition has no higher 60-day forward return than cases where only one series is positive, or if the interaction term is not positive in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A downward break in INDPRO indicates weakening production and inventory adjustment, which lowers near-term revenue expectations for cyclical firms and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its trailing 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:2013-10-15 snapshot INDPRO was 99.3579 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside crossings are followed by nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if downside crossings do not underperform upside crossings by at least 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: A positive trailing trend in HOUST signals stronger construction activity, durable-goods demand, and household balance-sheet confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:2013-10-15 snapshot HOUST was 891 thousand units as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive 6-month HOUST momentum does not predict higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative HOUST momentum, or if the slope coefficient is negative in most walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while UNRATE remains elevated and FEDFUNDS stays near zero, inflation pressure reduces real household purchasing power without confirming strong growth, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month CPIAUCSL growth accelerates, UNRATE is above 7 percent, and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent
evidence_refs: fact:2013-10-15 snapshot CPIAUCSL was 233.526, UNRATE was 7.3, and FEDFUNDS was 0.08
failure_condition: Reject if this three-series condition is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60 percent of triggered observations or if CPI acceleration is not associated with lower returns conditional on high UNRATE and low FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A real GDP growth acceleration above trend raises aggregate sales and operating leverage expectations, causing investors to mark up earnings forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses above 2 percent
evidence_refs: fact:2013-10-15 snapshot GDPC1 was 15679.7 as of t-6mo
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth crossings above 2 percent do not precede higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than crossings below 2 percent, or if the positive-growth signal has negative average returns in two consecutive expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A positive PAYEMS surprise trend, measured by a rising 3-month average payroll gain, signals stronger labor income and reduces recession probability, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average PAYEMS change crosses above its trailing 12-month average change
evidence_refs: fact:2013-10-15 snapshot PAYEMS was 136133 thousand persons as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS acceleration crossings have nonpositive average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if the hit rate of positive 5-day returns after crossings is below 50 percent in walk-forward testing.
